name: Makapuu Tide Pools
tagline: Natural lava rock tide pools teeming with sea life — free outdoor aquarium for curious keiki.
parking: Park at Makapuu Lookout lot (free) or along Kalanianaole Hwy near the pools
hours: Open 24 hours; best during daylight at low tide
bestTime: Low tide is essential — check tide charts. Morning for calmer conditions and fewer people
talkStory: These lava rock tide pools sit below the Makapuu Lighthouse Trail at the easternmost point of Oahu, formed by ancient volcanic flows meeting the ocean. At low tide, the pools trap small fish, hermit crabs, sea urchins, and colorful anemones -- a free natural aquarium. The area is also a prime whale-watching spot from November to April; humpback whales pass remarkably close to shore, making it a rare two-for-one nature experience.
